tail_risk_analysis.details: Near-Gaussian tails (kurtosis 0.1) — suspiciously thin; Symmetric distribution (skew 0.10); Asymmetric upside (tail ratio 1.35); Resilient (max drawdown -10.9%)

antifragility_analysis.details: Net debt but manageable relative to assets; Moderate leverage (D/E 0.43); Margins somewhat stable (CV 0.11) but low (avg 4.0%); Consistent FCF generation (10/10 periods positive)

convexity_analysis.details: R&D data not available — no penalty for non-R&D sectors; Slight positive asymmetry (up/down ratio 1.17); Low cash relative to market cap (1%); Low FCF yield (1.2%)

fragility_analysis.details: Low leverage (D/E 0.43) — not fragile; Interest coverage data not available; Moderate earnings volatility (growth std 0.20); Paper-thin margins (2.9%) — one shock away from loss

macro_context.market_sentiment: Market is cautious-to-mixed on April 29: S&P 500 flat (-0.04%), Dow down 5th straight day (-0.57% to 48,861), Nasdaq barely positive (+0.04%). Investors bracing for Magnificent Seven earnings (GOOGL, MSFT, AMZN, META reporting after bell) and a historic Fed decision. Sentiment pulled between AI optimism and geopolitical/energy headwinds.
macro_context.fed_outlook: FOMC held benchmark rate at 3.5%-3.75% on April 29, 2026 — third consecutive hold. Unusually divisive 8-4 vote (most dissent since October 1992), with four officials opposing any easing-bias language. Inflation remains elevated partly from rising global energy prices. Jerome Powell's final FOMC meeting as chair; Kevin Warsh expected to succeed him May 15 but Powell will remain on the Board.
macro_context.geopolitical: U.S.-Iran conflict is the dominant geopolitical risk: Iran closed the Strait of Hormuz (March 4), restricting ~20M barrels/day (~27% global maritime oil trade). Trump scrapped envoy peace talks and is reportedly preparing an extended blockade. Iran offered a new Hormuz deal but nuclear talks deferred. Goldman Sachs raised Brent forecast to $90/bbl by late 2026. Higher energy costs feed into consumer inflation and Walmart's operating costs.
key_events: Magnificent Seven earnings (GOOGL, MSFT, AMZN, META) reporting April 29 after bell — key AI/consumer sentiment read
Fed chair transition: Powell exits May 15, Warsh expected to take over — policy uncertainty elevated
U.S.-Iran Strait of Hormuz blockade — sustained oil price pressure
IMF downgraded global growth outlook citing war and trade conflicts
Walmart Q1 FY2027 earnings on May 14, 2026 — 15 days away

ticker_news.headline: Walmart releases 2026 Annual Report and Proxy Statement ahead of June 4 Annual Shareholders' Meeting; Tim Flynn retires from Board
Walmart redesigns leadership pipeline: eliminating 'store lead' role, expanding second-tier store managers with $85K-$276K compensation range
Walmart announces major U.S. store investment program to drive speed, convenience and growth — creating local jobs
Walmart unveils modern redesign of Great Value, its flagship private brand — moves to strengthen value positioning
Walmart pilots third-party marketplace backroom storage in Dallas-area stores to enable same-day delivery — direct challenge to Amazon

analyst_consensus.recent_changes: Consensus firmly bullish: 39 of 43 analysts rate Buy or Strong Buy (StockAnalysis, April 22, 2026). Average price target $133.86-$139.04 across sources (30-41 analysts, 3-month window); high $150, low $112. Notable downgrade: HSBC cut from Buy to Hold on February 20, 2026. Telsey Advisory maintained Outperform. FY2027 guidance ($2.75-$2.85 adj. EPS, +3.5-4.5% revenue) seen as conservatively set.

web_context.competitor_activity: Costco is the sharpest pricing threat: basket prices 21.4% below Walmart on groceries, 14% below on discretionary — gaining market share faster than any major rival as household budgets stay tight. Amazon is investing ~$200B CapEx in 2026 vs. Walmart's disciplined $25B; Walmart's U.S. eCommerce surged 27% YoY and now represents 23% of net sales, closing the gap. Target is struggling under new CEO Michael Fiddelke with shrinking sales and a beaten-down stock — ceding ground to both Walmart and Costco.
web_context.earnings_info: Next earnings: May 14, 2026 before market open (Q1 FY2027). FY2027 annual guidance: revenue +3.5%-4.5%, adjusted EPS $2.75-$2.85. CFO John David Rainey called guidance 'prudently conservative' given macro instability. EPS and revenue beat expectations in the most recent quarter. Specific Q1 FY2027 analyst EPS/revenue estimates not retrieved but consensus is broadly in line with company guidance. Sources: MarketBeat, TipRanks, Nasdaq.com.
